key_insight: The paper shows that agentic theorem-proving systems can build a machine-checked Lean formalization of Shor’s algorithm family and its RSA-2048 and P-256 attack resource estimates.
engineering_relevance: It demonstrates a path toward trustworthy AI-assisted verification of quantum algorithms and cryptanalytic resource analysis in formal proof assistants.
method: Used software agents to search sources, write Lean code, and repair proofs.
Human reviewers checked scientific claims while Lean machine-checked the formal proofs.
Formalized quantum order-finding, reversible arithmetic circuits, and resource estimates for RSA-2048 and P-256.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a completed Lean formalization with logical resource estimates derived from prior results for RSA-2048 and P-256, plus additional classical-operation estimates.
watch_item: The attack analyses depend on substantial formal modeling effort and resource-estimate assumptions, so proof correctness does not imply practical quantum capability.
paper_title: Building Shor's Algorithm in Lean: An Agentic Formalization of Quantum Attacks on RSA-2048 and P-256
